issues.comment: In Python dataclasses, default values are evaluated once when the class is defined (at module import time), not each time an instance is created. This means every AssignmentSource instance that relies on the default `queued` value will share the exact same timestamp — the time the module was first imported. Over the life of a long-running process, this timestamp will become increasingly stale and meaningless. The `from_integration` factory method doesn't pass `queued`, so it hits this default on every call. Fix: use `queued: datetime = field(default_factory=timezone.now)`.
